query:
You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 25.01 (commit: a24e5d1d).

## Issue to Resolve

[Reader IA] Show lists directly on dropdown menu if there are 2 or fewer
We are currently grouping the Lists in a "Lists" submenu independent of the number of lists the user has, but we should only show that submenu when the user has over 2 lists.

So if the user has 2 or fewer, they should be displayed directly in the menu root.

![image](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/57e663d9-218f-4a58-a447-05a86406ea86)

Internal ref: pcdRpT-4tr-p2#comment-7237

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/utils/ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
**Fixing menu logic**

I need to fix something in the ReaderTopBarMenuHelper. I plan to read the file and use some tools, but I'm not accessing git for this. My goal is to inspect how the menu is built, since it seems like lists in the submenu always pop up. I'll change the logic: if there are two or fewer lists, I'll add items to the root instead of the submenu. I need to ensure group IDs and ordering are correct. Let's open the file!

1‚Üípackage org.wordpress.android.ui.reader.utils
  2‚Üí
  3‚Üíimport androidx.collection.SparseArrayCompat
  4‚Üíimport androidx.collection.forEach
  5‚Üíimport androidx.collection.isNotEmpty
  6‚Üíimport androidx.collection.set
  7‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.R
  8‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.models.ReaderTag
  9‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.models.ReaderTagList
 10‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.models.ReaderTagType
 11‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.ui.compose.components.menu.dropdown.MenuElementData
 12‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiString
 13‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.util.config.ReaderTagsFeedFeatureConfig
 14‚Üíimport org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.indexOrNull
 15‚Üíimport javax.inject.Inject
 16‚Üí
 17‚Üíclass ReaderTopBarMenuHelper @Inject constructor(
 18‚Üí    private val readerTagsFeedFeatureConfig: ReaderTagsFeedFeatureConfig
 19‚Üí) {
 20‚Üí    fun createMenu(readerTagsList: ReaderTagList): List<MenuElementData> {
 21‚Üí        return mutableListOf<MenuElementData>().apply {
 22‚Üí            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isDiscover }?.let { discoverIndex ->
 23‚Üí                add(createDiscoverItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(discoverIndex)))
 24‚Üí            }
 25‚Üí            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isFollowedSites }?.let { followingIndex ->
 26‚Üí                add(createSubscriptionsItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(followingIndex)))
 27‚Üí            }
 28‚Üí            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isBookmarked }?.let { savedIndex ->
 29‚Üí                add(createSavedItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(savedIndex)))
 30‚Üí            }
 31‚Üí            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isPostsILike }?.let { likedIndex ->
 32‚Üí                add(createLikedItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(likedIndex)))
 33‚Üí            }
 34‚Üí            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isA8C }?.let { a8cIndex ->
 35‚Üí                add(createAutomatticItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(a8cIndex)))
 36‚Üí            }
 37‚Üí            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isP2 }?.let { followedP2sIndex ->
 38‚Üí                add(createFollowedP2sItem(
 39‚Üí                    id = getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(followedP2sIndex),
 40‚Üí                    text = readerTagsList[followedP2sIndex].tagTitle,
 41‚Üí                ))
 42‚Üí            }
 43‚Üí            if (readerTagsFeedFeatureConfig.isEnabled()) {
 44‚Üí                readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isTags }?.let { tagsIndex ->
 45‚Üí                    add(createTagsItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(tagsIndex)))
 46‚Üí                }
 47‚Üí            }
 48‚Üí            readerTagsList
 49‚Üí                .foldIndexed(SparseArrayCompat<ReaderTag>()) { index, sparseArray, readerTag ->
 50‚Üí                    if (readerTag.tagType == ReaderTagType.CUSTOM_LIST) {
 51‚Üí                        sparseArray[index] = readerTag
 52‚Üí                    }
 53‚Üí                    sparseArray
 54‚Üí                }
 55‚Üí                .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
 56‚Üí                ?.let { customListsArray ->
 57‚Üí                    add(MenuElementData.Divider)
 58‚Üí                    add(createCustomListsItem(customListsArray))
 59‚Üí                }
 60‚Üí        }
 61‚Üí    }
 62‚Üí
 63‚Üí    private fun createDiscoverItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 64‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 65‚Üí            id = id,
 66‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_discover),
 67‚Üí            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_discover_24dp,
 68‚Üí        )
 69‚Üí    }
 70‚Üí
 71‚Üí    private fun createSubscriptionsItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 72‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 73‚Üí            id = id,
 74‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_subscriptions),
 75‚Üí            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_subscriptions_24dp,
 76‚Üí        )
 77‚Üí    }
 78‚Üí
 79‚Üí    private fun createSavedItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 80‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 81‚Üí            id = id,
 82‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_saved),
 83‚Üí            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_saved_24dp,
 84‚Üí        )
 85‚Üí    }
 86‚Üí
 87‚Üí    private fun createLikedItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 88‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 89‚Üí            id = id,
 90‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_liked),
 91‚Üí            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_liked_24dp,
 92‚Üí        )
 93‚Üí    }
 94‚Üí
 95‚Üí    private fun createAutomatticItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 96‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 97‚Üí            id = id,
 98‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_automattic),
 99‚Üí        )
100‚Üí    }
101‚Üí
102‚Üí    private fun createFollowedP2sItem(id: String, text: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
103‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
104‚Üí            id = id,
105‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringText(text),
106‚Üí        )
107‚Üí    }
108‚Üí
109‚Üí    private fun createTagsItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
110‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
111‚Üí            id = id,
112‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_tags),
113‚Üí            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_tags_24dp,
114‚Üí        )
115‚Üí    }
116‚Üí
117‚Üí    private fun createCustomListsItem(customLists: SparseArrayCompat<ReaderTag>): MenuElementData.Item.SubMenu {
118‚Üí        val customListsMenuItems = mutableListOf<MenuElementData.Item.Single>()
119‚Üí        customLists.forEach { index, readerTag ->
120‚Üí            customListsMenuItems.add(
121‚Üí                MenuElementData.Item.Single(
122‚Üí                    id = getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(index),
123‚Üí                    text = UiString.UiStringText(readerTag.tagTitle),
124‚Üí                )
125‚Üí            )
126‚Üí        }
127‚Üí        return MenuElementData.Item.SubMenu(
128‚Üí            // We don't need this ID since this menu item just opens the sub-menu. It doesn't
129‚Üí            // change the content that is currently being displayed.
130‚Üí            id = "custom-lists",
131‚Üí            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_lists),
132‚Üí            children = customListsMenuItems,
133‚Üí        )
134‚Üí    }
135‚Üí
136‚Üí    private fun getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(readerTagIndex: Int): String = "$readerTagIndex"
137‚Üí
138‚Üí    fun getReaderTagIndexFromMenuItem(menuItem: MenuElementData.Item.Single) = menuItem.id.toInt()
139‚Üí}
140‚Üí

content.thinking:
**Clarifying SparseArrayCompat size**

I need to confirm the size method for SparseArrayCompat in Java and Kotlin. In Java, I see that there‚Äôs a size() method, while Kotlin might treat it as a property. I think it‚Äôs simplest to use customListsArray.size(), since SparseArrayCompat has a function size(), but Kotlin sees Java methods as functions. I‚Äôll check to see if there‚Äôs an extension property, but I‚Äôll safely use .size(). Additionally, I‚Äôll note we‚Äôre currently building SparseArrayCompat using foldIndexed, and that‚Äôs fine.

I see a potential issue with SparseArrayCompat.size() being a Java method, while in Kotlin, it‚Äôs just size(). I'll keep using size() since in Kotlin it returns an Int. content.text:
Implemented conditional display for Reader custom lists in `ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt`.

- If the user has **2 or fewer** custom lists, they are now added **directly to the root** dropdown menu (after the divider), with no ‚ÄúLists‚Äù submenu.
- If the user has **more than 2** custom lists, the existing **‚ÄúLists‚Äù submenu** behavior remains unchanged.
